summary: Permission-based sidebar navigation feature tested. All 5 core test cases passed. Admin sees Users link (role-based), CustomOps sees Users link (permission-based via admin.manage_users), Intern does NOT see Users link (no admin perms). CustomOps can access /users page and Create User dialog works.

minor.issue: CustomOps user with admin.manage_users permission gets 'Failed to load users' error when viewing Users page. The user list doesn't load, but Create User functionality works.

passed_tests: 1. Admin user sees Users link in sidebar (role-based) - PASSED
2. CustomOps user (OperationLead role with admin.manage_users) sees Users link in sidebar (permission-based) - PASSED
3. Intern user (Designer role, no admin perms) does NOT see Users link in sidebar - PASSED
4. CustomOps user can access /users page - PASSED
5. CustomOps user sees Create User button - PASSED
6. CustomOps user can open Create User dialog and fill form - PASSED
action_items: Investigate why GET /api/users returns error for CustomOps user with admin.manage_users permission - the user list doesn't load but Create User works
critical_code_review_comments: Sidebar.jsx correctly implements permission-based navigation at lines 314-329
hasPermission checks for admin.manage_users OR admin.assign_permissions to show Users link
AuthContext.js hasPermission function correctly checks effective_permissions array
Users.jsx correctly uses hasPermission for access control and showing Create User button
seed_data_creation: Fixed intern user password hash in database for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Permission-based sidebar navigation is working correctly. Sidebar.jsx checks hasPermission('admin.manage_users') || hasPermission('admin.assign_permissions') to add Users link for non-admin users. Test users: Admin (thaha.pakayil@gmail.com), CustomOps (test.customops@arkiflo.com - has admin.manage_users), Intern (test.intern@arkiflo.com - no admin perms). Note: GET /api/users may have permission issues for non-Admin users.
